title: Engagement Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Status reporting, SOW and deliverable tracking, and standard client update decks are now largely AI-drafted, but de-escalating an at-risk client relationship or renegotiating scope mid-engagement stays firmly human.
rationales.toolMaturity: Contract and SOW drafting assistants (Ironclad AI and similar) plus generalist LLMs cover the paperwork well, but there is no mature, purpose-built 'engagement management' product category yet.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted reporting and contract drafting is common in consulting and tech engagement teams, but adoption is uneven outside those sectors.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can already draft status updates and flag deliverable slippage from project data, but they can't read a client's frustration in a room and adjust the relationship in real time.

tasksAtRisk: Client status reporting and update decks
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1111 Management Analysts (closest detailed occupation for client-facing engagement/delivery leadership), p25-p75 rounded, upper end extended for tech/consulting market postings

defenseSkills.skill: AI Contract & SOW Drafting Tools (Ironclad AI)
Agentic AI Literacy for Delivery Operations

insight: AI now drafts the status decks and flags slipping deliverables before an engagement manager even opens the project tracker. What still requires a human is reading a client's mood in a steering committee meeting and knowing when to eat a scope change instead of billing for it.
